OFFER TO COMPROMISE AND ACCEPTANCE OF OFFER TO COMPROMISE FILED BY PLAINTIFF SHEFFER, GREGORY M. ,INDIVIDUALS SHEFFER, HEIDI J. ,INDIVIDUALS AS TO DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. AND THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED. FIRST, PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE SUBMITTED THE COSTS ISSUE TO THE ARBITRATOR PURSUANT TO CORONA V. AMHERST PARTNERS (2003) 107 CAL.APP.4TH 701. SECOND, THE PARTIES AGREED THAT EACH PARTY WOULD BEAR ITS OWN COSTS "REGARDLESS OF THE 'OUTCOME OF THE ARBITRATION." THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT OR THE PARTIES REPUDIATED THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

NOTICE OF PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION ; APPENDIX FILED BY DEFENDANT KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC. HEARING SET FOR OCT-30-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
JURY FEES DEPOSITED BY PLAINTIFF SHEFFER, GREGORY M. ,INDIVIDUALS

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. AND THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT PLAINTIFF HEIDI SHEFFER ENTERED INTO AN AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE WITH DEFENDANTS, PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE CONTRACT IS BOTH PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE, THE AGREEMENT COMPLIES WITH HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE 1363.1, AND NON-SIGNATORY PLAINTIFF GREGORY SHEFFER IS BOUND TO ARBITRATE HIS CLAIMS UNDER RUIZ V. PODOLSKY (2010) 50 CAL.4TH 838. IN ADDITION, THE COURT OVERRULES PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATIONS OF JOHN KRESICH, DEBORAH ESPINAL AND KAREN LEWIS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT BY EMAIL, WITH COPY TO OPPOSING COUNSEL.) JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Ntc Of Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jun-13-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory Sheffer, Esq., 415.388.0911, for plaintiffs Heidi Sheffer and Gregory Sheffer.
James Allen, Esq., 925.939.0203, for defendants Kaiser Foundation Hospitals, Kaiser Foundation Health Plan Inc. and the Permanente Medical Group, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. AND THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC'S Motion To Tax Costs is granted. First, plaintiff should have submitted the costs issue to the arbitrator pursuant to Corona v. Amherst Partners (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 701. Second, the parties agreed that each party would bear its own costs "regardless of the 'outcome of the arbitration." There is no evidence that defendant or the parties repudiated the arbitration agreement. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Appearances:
Yvonne M. Pierrou, Esq. of Marion's Inn LLP, Tel. 510.451.6770, for defendants and moving party;
Gregory M. Sheffer, Esq. of Sheffer Law Firm, Tel. 415.388.0911, appearing on his own behalf and for plaintiffs and opposing party.

The matter was argued. RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. and THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is granted. The Court concludes that plaintiff Heidi Sheffer entered into an agreement to arbitrate with defendants, plaintiffs fail to demonstrate that the contract is both procedurally and substantively unconscionable, the agreement complies with Health and Safety Code 1363.1, and non-signatory plaintiff Gregory Sheffer is bound to arbitrate his claims under Ruiz v. Podolsky (2010) 50 Cal.4th 838. In addition, the court overrules plaintiff's evidentiary objections to the declarations of John Kresich, Deborah Espinal and Karen Lewis. (Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order and submit it to the court by email, with copy to opposing counsel.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
